summary: Focused frontend test of VirusTotal trust bar Link click-through. All 3 scenarios PASS: (1) Landing -> click 'trust-virustotal' navigates to /security#faq-av and auto-opens the antivirus FAQ (faq-item-1) with faq-vt-link visible and faq-av in viewport (top=465px). (2) On /security same-page click updates hash to #faq-av, opens FAQ, and scrolls. (3) Direct load of /security#faq-av auto-opens FAQ on mount. faq-vt-link is a proper <a target='_blank' href='https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/8460ed1d73dbaa6415e2ab9035293d411639fa8642e44d46147043dfc372130c'>. The bug reported by user ('non succede nulla') is NOT reproducible — behavior works as designed.
action_items: No code changes required — VirusTotal trust bar click-through is fully functional. Consider asking the user to hard-refresh / clear service worker cache if they still see the old external-link behavior (cached bundle).
critical_code_review_comments: TrustBar.jsx correctly renders VirusTotal item as react-router <Link to='/security#faq-av'> (rendered as <a href='/security#faq-av'>).
SecurityFaq.jsx useLocation-based useEffect properly handles both cross-page navigation and same-page hash change — verified.
Non-blocking noise: two 401 responses from /api/auth/me for anonymous visitors (expected, as noted in request).
context_for_next_testing_agent: VirusTotal badge click-through confirmed working across all three entry points (landing click, security same-page click, direct hash URL). URL before landing click: '/'. URL after: '/security#faq-av'. faq-vt-link href points to raw VirusTotal report with target=_blank as required.
rca of the issue: No bug found. The recent change from external href to internal <Link to='/security#faq-av'> plus useLocation hash effect in SecurityFaq works as intended. User's 'non succede nulla' report is likely due to browser/service-worker caching of the previous build.
